---
title: "Probability & ML Foundations"
sidebar: false
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.hero-section}

::: {.hero-eyebrow}
A 12-Week Series for Business Professionals
:::

::: {.hero-headline}
The Math Behind Every Business Decision You've Already Made
:::

::: {.hero-subtitle}
You already think in probabilities, conditional risks, and anomalies. This series makes that thinking rigorous — probability and statistics for **internal auditors, financial analysts, and fraud examiners** who want to build and understand ML models, not just run them.
:::

::: {.hero-actions}
[Start with Week 1 →](content/en/week-01-conditioning.qmd){.btn .btn-primary .btn-lg}
[See the full series](#the-series){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-lg}
:::

:::

---

## Who This Is For

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-4}

**⚖️ Internal Auditors**

You already prioritize risks, evaluate evidence, and update your judgment as findings come in. Bayes' theorem is how you already think — this series puts the formula behind it so you can build audit risk models, explain them to stakeholders, and defend them to regulators.

:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-4}

**📊 Financial Analysts**

You log-transform skewed revenue data by instinct and model correlated KPIs in spreadsheets. This series shows you the probability theory that justifies what you already do — and extends it to models that handle 50 variables, not 5.

:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-4}

**🔍 Fraud Examiners**

You detect patterns, score anomalies, and update risk assessments as evidence accumulates. Markov chains, entropy, and kernel methods are already part of your investigation logic — this series makes the math explicit so you can build models that scale across thousands of transactions.

:::
:::

:::

---

## The Series {#the-series}

12 weeks of probability and statistics — each grounded in a business problem you've already faced. **April – July 2026.**

::: {.card .series-live-card .p-4 .mt-3 .mb-4}

::: {.d-flex .justify-content-between .align-items-center .mb-2}
::: {.series-week-label}
WEEK 1 · APR 13–19
:::
::: {.badge-live}
Live
:::
:::

**Probability Conditioning & Bayes**

*Given a \$10K invoice from a new vendor — what's the real fraud probability? Every auditor already does this calculation. Here's the formula behind it.*

Conditional probability · Bayes' theorem · Law of total probability

[Open Week 1 →](content/en/week-01-conditioning.qmd){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-secondary .mt-2}

:::

*New lessons published weekly.*

---

## How Each Notebook Is Structured

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-sm-6 .g-col-lg-3}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-4}
::: {.step-number}
1
:::
**The Business Problem**

A real scenario from audit, finance, or fraud — something you've already encountered in practice.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-sm-6 .g-col-lg-3}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-4}
::: {.step-number}
2
:::
**The Solution**

A working Python implementation that solves the problem — before the theory.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-sm-6 .g-col-lg-3}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-4}
::: {.step-number}
3
:::
**The Math**

The probability or statistics that explains *why* the solution works — with plain-English descriptions alongside every equation.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-sm-6 .g-col-lg-3}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-4}
::: {.step-number}
4
:::
**Exercises**

Scenarios drawn from audit sampling, fraud investigation, and financial modeling — to test the concept on problems you actually face.
:::
:::

:::
